flashcards.definition: to find or meet something/someone by chance, unexpectedly
flashcards.translation: se deparar com, encontrar por acaso
flashcards.example: I came across a great article about English learning.

flashcards.definition: to stay informed about something; to maintain the same pace as someone or something
flashcards.translation: acompanhar, manter-se atualizado sobre
flashcards.example: It is hard to keep up with the news every day.

flashcards.definition: to stop working (machine/vehicle); to separate something into smaller parts; to lose emotional control
flashcards.translation: quebrar, parar de funcionar; dividir em partes; desabar emocionalmente

flashcards.definition: to quickly pack your luggage and put it in a vehicle, ready for a trip
flashcards.translation: jogar as malas (no carro), despachar a bagagem rapidamente

flashcards.definition: the storage compartment at the back of a car (American English); called 'boot' in British English
flashcards.translation: porta-malas (do carro)

flashcards.definition: to fill an insulated container with ice and food/drinks to keep them cold during a trip
flashcards.translation: encher uma caixa térmica com gelo e bebidas/comidas

flashcards.definition: a long, clear stretch of road with no traffic, symbolizing freedom and adventure
flashcards.translation: estrada aberta, rodovia livre — símbolo de liberdade e aventura

flashcards.definition: to end up in a place or situation, often unexpectedly; also to finish or conclude something
flashcards.translation: acabar em, terminar em (um lugar ou situação inesperada); encerrar algo

flashcards.definition: varied, showing a great deal of variety; having many different kinds (note: likely a typo for 'diverse')
flashcards.translation: diverso, variado, com muitos tipos diferentes
flashcards.example: Streaming is also opening the door for more diverse artists and fans.

flashcards.definition: to find or follow the history of something back to its beginning or source
flashcards.translation: rastrear a origem, investigar de onde algo vem
flashcards.example: You can trace the origin of this love story back one hundred years exactly, to the founding of Route 66.

flashcards.definition: to join separate pieces or parts into a whole, like sewing fabric together
flashcards.translation: costurado junto, unido — como peças separadas formando um todo
flashcards.example: Private associations stitched together roads and marketed them to travelers, all to help improve business along the road.

flashcards.definition: to become involved in a situation, especially to help or take control
flashcards.translation: intervir, entrar em ação para ajudar ou assumir o controle
flashcards.example: But in the early 1920s, the federal government stepped in to standardize national roadways.

flashcards.definition: to make things uniform, following the same rules or model (note: typo for 'standardize')
flashcards.translation: padronizar, uniformizar, seguir um mesmo modelo ou regra
flashcards.example: But in the early 1920s, the federal government stepped in to standardize national roadways.

flashcards.definition: logical, consistent, and easy to understand; forming a unified whole
flashcards.translation: coerente, lógico, fácil de entender
flashcards.example: Now, they would have a coherent, easy-to-use system of national highways.

flashcards.definition: past tense of 'strike'; to hit or affect something suddenly and with force (e.g. a disaster strikes a region)
flashcards.translation: atingiu, golpeou — passado de 'strike'; afetou de forma súbita
flashcards.example: The Dust Bowl was a ten-year environmental disaster that struck the Great Plains, in the middle of the country.

flashcards.definition: an extreme and prolonged period with little or no rainfall, causing serious damage
flashcards.translation: seca severa, período prolongado de falta de chuva
flashcards.example: So when a severe drought hit in the early 1930s, massive windstorms blew the loose topsoil all over the region, covering towns and farms in thick dust.

flashcards.definition: a heavy layer of fine dry particles covering surfaces, often from wind or dry conditions
flashcards.translation: poeira densa, camada grossa de partículas finas no ar ou nas superfícies
flashcards.example: So when a severe drought hit in the early 1930s, massive windstorms blew the loose topsoil all over the region, covering towns and farms in thick dust.

flashcards.definition: to happen at the same time as something else
flashcards.translation: coincidiu com, aconteceu ao mesmo tempo que
flashcards.example: The Dust Bowl coincided with an economic depression.

flashcards.definition: completely covered with a hard surface (asphalt or concrete), making it suitable for vehicles
flashcards.translation: completamente asfaltado, com pavimentação total
flashcards.example: In 1938, Route 66 became the first fully paved highway in the United States.

flashcards.definition: turned out to be helpful or practical in a real situation
flashcards.translation: se mostrou útil, revelou-se prático em uma situação real
flashcards.example: That proved useful just a few years later during World War II, when large trucks transported military munitions from the ports in California to the factories in the Midwest and the eastern United States.

flashcards.definition: large buildings where goods are manufactured or assembled using machines and workers
flashcards.translation: fábricas, indústrias
flashcards.example: That proved useful just a few years later during World War II, when large trucks transported military munitions from the ports in California to the factories in the Midwest and the eastern United States.
contexts: Many factories moved out of the city center to reduce operating costs and avoid congestion.
The industrial revolution transformed small workshops into large factories with hundreds of workers.

flashcards.definition: a large amount of money, assets, or valuable possessions; prosperity
flashcards.translation: riqueza, prosperidade, grande quantidade de bens ou dinheiro
flashcards.example: After World War II, Americans had the wealth to own their own cars, and the family road trip became a popular option for middle-class vacations.

flashcards.definition: glowing signs made with neon gas in glass tubes, commonly used by businesses for advertising
flashcards.translation: letreiros de néon, placas luminosas coloridas usadas em estabelecimentos
flashcards.example: Route 66 transformed itself into a linear tourist destination, with neon signs, roadside attractions, campgrounds, and family-friendly motels with swimming pools.

flashcards.definition: escaped from the devastating drought by moving away from the affected area
flashcards.translation: fugiu da seca, escapou da região devastada pela estiagem
flashcards.example: It was called the “Main Street of America.” John Steinbeck’s famous novel, “The Grapes of Wrath” traced a Dust Bowl-era family as it fled the drought along Route 66.
contexts: Thousands of farming families fled the drought and headed west in search of fertile land.
Many communities fled the drought that had devastated their crops for two consecutive years.

flashcards.definition: to drive westward; to travel by car towards the west
flashcards.translation: dirigir em direção ao oeste, viajar de carro para o oeste
flashcards.example: “If you ever plan to motor west,” he sings, “Take the highway that’s the best.” Later, a TV show called simply “Route 66” brought the road into American living rooms every week.

flashcards.definition: not anymore; used to say that something which was true in the past is not true now
flashcards.translation: não mais, já não — algo que era verdade no passado mas não é mais
flashcards.example: Route 66 is no longer the best way to motor west, from Chicago to LA, like it was in Nat King Cole’s time.
contexts: I no longer drink coffee after six in the evening because it affects my sleep.
The company no longer produces that model since it was discontinued three years ago.

flashcards.definition: invented; not real; consisting of certain parts (note: typo for 'made up')
flashcards.translation: inventado, fictício; composto por certas partes
flashcards.example: But many of the individual sections of roadway that once made up Route 66 are still there—and some stretches remain popular with tourists.

flashcards.definition: extended over a long distance or period of time
flashcards.translation: se estendia, se espalhava por uma longa distância ou período

flashcards.definition: to clean, repair, and renovate a building or object to restore it to good condition
flashcards.translation: reformar, renovar, restaurar algo ao seu bom estado original
flashcards.example: The city of Albuquerque, New Mexico, is helping to pay to refurbish the neon signs on its 18 miles of Route 66.
contexts: The hotel was completely refurbished last year and now looks like a brand-new building.
The city plans to refurbish the old train station and turn it into a cultural center.

flashcards.definition: having its origins or basis in something; deeply connected to
flashcards.translation: enraizado em, tendo suas origens em
flashcards.example: Country is a genre rooted in acoustic instruments and stories about everyday life—love, loss, and small- town struggles.

flashcards.definition: difficulties, challenges, or hard efforts to achieve something
flashcards.translation: lutas, dificuldades, desafios do dia a dia
flashcards.example: Country is a genre rooted in acoustic instruments and stories about everyday life—love, loss, and small- town struggles.

flashcards.definition: located in, coming from, or characteristic of the south of a country or region
flashcards.translation: sulista, do sul, característico do sul de um país ou região
flashcards.example: It’s especially popular in the southern United States and in rural areas.

flashcards.definition: a person who bets money on games, races, or uncertain outcomes
flashcards.translation: jogador, apostador; pessoa que aposta dinheiro em jogos ou corridas
flashcards.example: Kenny Rogers sang about a veteran gambler giving advice to a fellow passenger on a lonely train ride.

flashcards.definition: used informally to mean a person; also an adjective meaning 'sharing the same activity or situation' (e.g. a fellow passenger)
flashcards.translation: sujeito, cara; também adjetivo: companheiro, colega (ex: fellow passenger = colega de viagem)
flashcards.example: Kenny Rogers sang about a veteran gambler giving advice to a fellow passenger on a lonely train ride.

flashcards.definition: to go away from a place or person; to not take someone or something with you when you leave
flashcards.translation: deixar para trás, não levar consigo ao partir
flashcards.example: Dolly Parton said goodbye to someone she loved but had to leave behind.

flashcards.definition: the broadcasting of a song or music on the radio or on a streaming platform
flashcards.translation: execução em rádio ou plataformas de streaming; tempo de antena para músicas
flashcards.example: Only a small percentage of “country” songs get airplay on pop or rock stations.
contexts: The new single got massive airplay on radio stations across the country within its first week.
Without airplay, it is very difficult for an independent artist to reach a wide audience.

flashcards.definition: companies that produce and distribute music recordings (e.g. Universal Music, Sony Music)
flashcards.translation: gravadoras, selos musicais — empresas que produzem e distribuem música
flashcards.example: All the major country music labels are headquartered along Nashville’s Music Row.

flashcards.definition: having the main office or center of operations located in a specific place
flashcards.translation: sediado em, tendo a sede principal localizada em determinado lugar
flashcards.example: All the major country music labels are headquartered along Nashville’s Music Row.
contexts: The company is headquartered in New York but has regional offices in over twenty countries.
Many of the world's largest technology firms are headquartered in Silicon Valley, California.

flashcards.definition: selling all available tickets or products; also informally, abandoning one's principles for commercial success
flashcards.translation: esgotando ingressos/produtos; popularmente: abrir mão dos princípios por dinheiro
flashcards.example: You can certainly find country music stars selling out arenas.

flashcards.definition: outdoor events held in rural areas with games, farm animals, food stalls, and entertainment
flashcards.translation: feiras rurais, festas de interior com animais, comidas e entretenimento

flashcards.definition: origins or background connected to the countryside, farming communities, and small-town life
flashcards.translation: raízes rurais, origem ligada ao campo e ao interior
flashcards.example: They’re proud of their rural roots, and they like having music that reflects their values and lifestyles.

flashcards.definition: extremely devoted and committed to a person, cause or value — defending it with great intensity and unwavering dedication (note: typo for 'fiercely loyal')
flashcards.translation: ferozmente leal — ter um compromisso profundo e inabalável com pessoas, causas ou valores. Envolve dedicação, proteção intensa e apoio constante.
flashcards.example: They’re fiercely loyal to their favorite artists, following them through long, multi-decade careers.

flashcards.definition: broader; affecting or reaching a larger group of people or area
flashcards.translation: mais amplo, mais abrangente; que alcança um público ou área maior
flashcards.example: Country tends to grab the wider music world’s attention when stars cross over into pop and rock.

flashcards.definition: to move from one genre, field, or category to another (e.g. a country artist crossing over to pop)
flashcards.translation: fazer crossover, migrar de um gênero musical (ou área) para outro
flashcards.example: Country tends to grab the wider music world’s attention when stars cross over into pop and rock.

flashcards.definition: arrived at; achieved; got to a certain level (e.g. reaching the top of the charts)
flashcards.translation: alcançou, chegou a — atingiu um determinado nível ou resultado
flashcards.example: In the 1990s, for example, country singers Garth Brooks and Shania Twain reached the top of the charts in both country and pop formats.

flashcards.definition: ranked lists of the most popular songs or albums, based on sales and streaming numbers
flashcards.translation: paradas musicais, rankings das músicas e álbuns mais populares
flashcards.example: The genre has its own charts and dedicated radio stations.

flashcards.definition: much further than; extending far past a certain limit or boundary
flashcards.translation: muito além de, bem além dos limites ou fronteiras tradicionais
flashcards.example: Country music is reaching new audiences, and some of its biggest songs are breaking out well beyond the traditional fan base.

flashcards.definition: gradually changed and developed into something different over time
flashcards.translation: evoluiu para, transformou-se gradualmente em algo diferente
flashcards.example: Before, it was only country stars who evolved into pop stars.
contexts: Country music evolved into a mainstream genre that appeals to audiences far beyond rural America.
What started as a small personal blog evolved into a successful media company with millions of readers.

flashcards.definition: to make a song, album, or product available to the public
flashcards.translation: lançar (uma música, álbum ou produto) ao público
flashcards.example: Now, though, some artists are going the other way: they start as pop stars, and then release a country album.

flashcards.definition: used to express that something was unlikely or would probably not happen under normal circumstances
flashcards.translation: talvez nunca, provavelmente não faria isso em circunstâncias normais
flashcards.example: It introduced country themes to millions of music fans who might never have given country a second thought.

flashcards.definition: to give something 'a second thought' means to reconsider it or think about it more carefully (note: typo for 'second thought')
flashcards.translation: dar uma segunda olhada, reconsiderar algo; pensar novamente antes de decidir
flashcards.example: It introduced country themes to millions of music fans who might never have given country a second thought.

flashcards.definition: continued and eventually achieved something after the previous action
flashcards.translation: continuou e posteriormente alcançou algo; passou a fazer
flashcards.example: “Cowboy Carter” went on to win the Grammy Award for Album of the Year in 2025.

flashcards.definition: before a particular time or event; ahead of time
flashcards.translation: com antecedência, antes de um determinado evento ou prazo
flashcards.example: Lana Del Rey has released two country songs in advance of an all-country album expected soon.

flashcards.definition: to have great success or achieve something very lucky, often unexpectedly
flashcards.translation: ganhar na loteria; ter um grande sucesso inesperado
flashcards.example: Luke Combs hit the jackpot when his interpretation of “Fast Car” became an international hit.

flashcards.definition: not in any other situation or circumstances; used to describe something that wouldn't happen normally
flashcards.translation: não de outra forma; que não aconteceria em circunstâncias normais
flashcards.example: Streaming algorithms often help fans of pop, rock, and R&B discover country songs they would not otherwise have had exposure to.

flashcards.definition: satisfies or makes happy; to please an algorithm means to create content that performs well on platforms
flashcards.translation: agrada, satisfaz; 'agradar o algoritmo' = criar conteúdo que performa bem nas plataformas
flashcards.example: But streaming also puts pressure on artists to engage with their fans on social media and to make music that pleases the algorithms.

flashcards.definition: any drink, especially one that is not water (e.g. coffee, tea, juice)
flashcards.translation: bebida — qualquer líquido para consumo, exceto água pura
flashcards.example: In this story, we’ll take a world tour of the different ways to produce the world’s second-most-popular hot beverage.

flashcards.definition: past tense of 'pour'; to cause a liquid to flow from a container into another
flashcards.translation: despejou, serviu um líquido de um recipiente para outro
flashcards.example: They boiled water, poured it over coffee grounds, waited for the grounds to settle to the bottom, and sipped carefully.

flashcards.definition: the solid residue of coffee beans left after brewing; the small particles of ground coffee
flashcards.translation: borra de café, resíduo sólido do café moído após o preparo
flashcards.example: They boiled water, poured it over coffee grounds, waited for the grounds to settle to the bottom, and sipped carefully.

flashcards.definition: drank slowly and in small amounts, with caution
flashcards.translation: bebericou com cuidado, tomou pequenos goles com atenção
flashcards.example: They boiled water, poured it over coffee grounds, waited for the grounds to settle to the bottom, and sipped carefully.

flashcards.definition: to drink something slowly, taking small amounts at a time
flashcards.translation: beber aos goles, tomar de pouquinho em pouquinho

flashcards.definition: a method of brewing coffee where hot water drips slowly through ground coffee into a pot or cup; also called filter coffee
flashcards.translation: café coado, café filtrado — o método em que a água quente passa pela borra com ajuda da gravidade
flashcards.example: That became what we call “filter coffee” or “drip coffee.” Unlike espresso, which forces hot water under pressure, filter coffee just uses gravity.

flashcards.definition: untidy, dirty, or disorganized; complicated and difficult to deal with
flashcards.translation: bagunçado, sujo, desorganizado; complicado e difícil de lidar

flashcards.definition: an unpleasant rough, sandy texture in your mouth when drinking or eating
flashcards.translation: gosto arenoso, sensação áspera e granulada na boca
flashcards.example: People didn’t like the gritty taste of grounds in the bottom of their cups, and it took a long time to make.

flashcards.definition: reduced to very small particles, almost like powder
flashcards.translation: finamente moído, triturado até virar quase um pó
flashcards.example: The machine forced hot water under pressure through finely-ground coffee.

flashcards.definition: groups of twelve; informally used to mean 'a large number' of something
flashcards.translation: dúzias; informalmente: grande quantidade de algo
flashcards.example: First, speed—a café could serve dozens of customers an hour instead of making them wait.

flashcards.definition: difficult; requiring a lot of effort; also strong and durable
flashcards.translation: difícil, trabalhoso; resistente, durável; também: severo, duro

flashcards.definition: having a sharp, unpleasant taste (like dark coffee or dark chocolate); also feeling angry or resentful
flashcards.translation: amargo (sabor); também: ressentido, amargurado emocionalmente
flashcards.example: That meant it could extract rich flavor from the beans without turning bitter like old boiled coffee.

flashcards.definition: to gradually reach a wider area or more people over time
flashcards.translation: espalhou-se, difundiu-se por uma área ou grupo maior ao longo do tempo
flashcards.example: The style spread across Europe and eventually around the world.

flashcards.definition: past tense of 'fold'; to bend something over itself (e.g. folding paper into a cone shape)
flashcards.translation: dobrou — dobrando o papel para formar uma forma (ex: um cone)
flashcards.example: So she took a page out of her son’s school notebook and folded it into a cone.
contexts: She folded the paper carefully and slid it inside the envelope before sealing it.
He folded a piece of filter paper into a cone and placed it over the cup.

flashcards.definition: made by soaking ingredients in hot liquid (coffee or tea); past tense of 'brew'
flashcards.translation: preparado, coado — passado de 'brew'; feito por infusão em líquido quente
flashcards.example: She put the coffee grounds on top of the paper, poured hot water over the grounds, and watched as a brewed beverage dripped out the bottom and into her cup.

flashcards.definition: falls in small drops; flows slowly, drop by drop
flashcards.translation: pinga, escorre gota a gota
flashcards.example: Hot water passes over ground coffee beans, and drips into a cup or a pot.

flashcards.definition: used to introduce a choice between two alternatives or to express a condition (note: typo for 'whether')
flashcards.translation: se, caso — usado para introduzir alternativas ou condições
flashcards.example: Whether you want to make just a single cup or fill a huge pot at a restaurant, the method is the same: hot water, grounds, filter, gravity.

flashcards.definition: having a consistency close to powder; extremely fine and dry
flashcards.translation: quase como um pó, com consistência extremamente fina e seca

flashcards.definition: thick, soft, wet mud or a similar substance; here: the thick residue at the bottom of a coffee cup
flashcards.translation: lodo, borra grossa — resíduo espesso no fundo do copo de café
flashcards.example: Boil it all together, pour into small cups, and sip carefully—leaving the sludge at the bottom.

flashcards.definition: to beat a liquid quickly and vigorously to add air and make it frothy or creamy
flashcards.translation: bater vigorosamente um líquido para incorporar ar e deixá-lo cremoso
flashcards.example: Add just the first few drops of coffee as it brews, then whip it vigorously until it turns pale and creamy.

flashcards.definition: a mass of small bubbles formed in or on a liquid; the frothy top of a coffee drink
flashcards.translation: espuma — massa de pequenas bolhas formadas sobre um líquido
flashcards.example: Pour the rest of the coffee over that foam.

flashcards.definition: rests or is positioned on the upper surface of something
flashcards.translation: fica por cima, está posicionado sobre a superfície de algo
flashcards.example: You put coffee grounds in a metal filter called a phin, which sits on top of your cup.

flashcards.definition: to say or write the letters of a word in order; past tense of 'spell'
flashcards.translation: soletrado — passado de 'spell'; dizer ou escrever as letras de uma palavra em ordem
flashcards.example: It’s spelled a-i-n, apostrophe, t.

flashcards.definition: different from; in contrast to
flashcards.translation: diferente de, ao contrário de, em contraste com
flashcards.example: But it was low-key : unlike jazz, which was high-energy , bossa nova was typically played at lower volumes and without a lot of drums and percussion instruments .

flashcards.definition: to speak or write to someone formally; also to deal with a problem
flashcards.translation: tratar alguém formalmente; dirigir-se a; também: endereço
flashcards.example: “Ma’am” is a formal way to address a woman.

flashcards.definition: suggests something without saying it directly; carries an indirect meaning
flashcards.translation: implica, sugere algo indiretamente sem dizer explicitamente
flashcards.example: It’s the female equivalent of the word “sir.” This can often be considered polite and respectful, but it always implies an emotional distance between two people.
contexts: Calling someone's feedback 'a bit harsh' implies that they may have gone too far.
The use of formal language in a letter implies respect and professionalism toward the reader.

flashcards.definition: to get out of a vehicle; also to leave a room briefly
flashcards.translation: sair do veículo; também: sair brevemente de um ambiente
flashcards.example: A police office might say, “Step out of the car please, ma’am.” A customer service agent might say, “Can I help you, ma’am?” But I would caution you against using “ma’am” unless you’re sure it’s appropriate.

flashcards.definition: to warn you not to do something; to advise against a particular action
flashcards.translation: advertir contra, aconselhar a não fazer algo
flashcards.example: A police office might say, “Step out of the car please, ma’am.” A customer service agent might say, “Can I help you, ma’am?” But I would caution you against using “ma’am” unless you’re sure it’s appropriate.

flashcards.definition: in opposition to; not in favor of; in contact with (physically)
flashcards.translation: contra, em oposição a; também: em contato físico com algo
flashcards.example: A police office might say, “Step out of the car please, ma’am.” A customer service agent might say, “Can I help you, ma’am?” But I would caution you against using “ma’am” unless you’re sure it’s appropriate.

flashcards.definition: a challenge, saying that you don't think someone will be brave enough to do something
flashcards.translation: desafio você, aposto que você não vai fazer isso
flashcards.example: I dare you to find me a funnier word!

flashcards.definition: the practice of measuring and recording time; keeping track of time accurately
flashcards.translation: marcação do tempo, controle de horários — a prática de medir e registrar o tempo
flashcards.example: The origin of “o’clock” goes back to the early days of mechanical timekeeping.

flashcards.definition: ancient devices that show the time by the shadow cast by sunlight on a marked surface
flashcards.translation: relógios de sol — dispositivos antigos que indicam as horas pela sombra da luz solar
flashcards.example: Before mechanical clocks, people kept time with sundials, hourglasses, and church bells—all of which were imprecise.
contexts: Before mechanical clocks existed, sundials were used to tell the time by reading the shadow of the sun.
The museum garden has a beautifully restored sundial dating back to the seventeenth century.

flashcards.definition: devices with two glass bulbs connected by a narrow neck through which sand falls to measure time
flashcards.translation: ampulhetas — dispositivos com areia que cai para medir o tempo
flashcards.example: Before mechanical clocks, people kept time with sundials, hourglasses, and church bells—all of which were imprecise.

flashcards.definition: large bells hung in church towers, rung to mark the hours or call people to services
flashcards.translation: sinos de igreja — usados para marcar as horas ou convocar fiéis
flashcards.example: Before mechanical clocks, people kept time with sundials, hourglasses, and church bells—all of which were imprecise.
contexts: Before mechanical clocks, church bells rang every hour so people could keep track of time.
We woke up to the sound of church bells echoing through the village on Sunday morning.

flashcards.definition: past tense of 'mean'; intended; was used for a particular purpose
flashcards.translation: significava, queria dizer; era destinado a; passado de 'mean'
flashcards.example: That meant it could extract rich flavor from the beans without turning bitter like old boiled coffee.

flashcards.definition: a level of quality or achievement considered normal or acceptable; an established rule or model (note: typo for 'standard')
flashcards.translation: padrão, norma, nível de qualidade aceito como referência
flashcards.example: The American jazz singer Stan Getz collaborated with Gilberto on an album called, imaginatively, “Getz/Gilberto,” which was a huge international success and included several songs that would become standard jazz songs of the era.
contexts: English has become the standard language for international business and academic communication.
The factory follows strict standard procedures to ensure consistent product quality at every stage.

flashcards.definition: difficult to deal with; requiring skill or caution; potentially deceptive
flashcards.translation: complicado, difícil de lidar; que exige cuidado; potencialmente enganoso

flashcards.definition: a rude or offensive word, often used to express strong emotions; a profanity or expletive
flashcards.translation: palavrão, palavrada — palavra rude ou ofensiva usada para expressar emoções fortes
flashcards.example: It’s not a swear word; it’s not profanity.

flashcards.definition: rude or offensive language, especially involving blasphemy or obscenity
flashcards.translation: profanidade, linguagem obscena ou blasfema
flashcards.example: It’s not a swear word; it’s not profanity.

flashcards.definition: expressed disapproval of; pointed out the faults or problems of something
flashcards.translation: criticado, censurado — apontado como errado ou inadequado
flashcards.example: It’s one of the few words that is common in daily speech, and yet is frequently criticized by authority figures like teachers and grammar experts.

flashcards.definition: not conforming to the accepted standard form of a language; considered informal or incorrect by grammar authorities
flashcards.translation: não padrão — que não segue as normas aceitas da língua; considerado informal ou incorreto
flashcards.example: Most authority figures in language say you shouldn’t use the word “ain’t.” Dictionaries call it “nonstandard.” Teachers tell you not to use it.
contexts: Using 'ain't' is considered nonstandard English and is generally avoided in formal writing.
The technical report was sent back because it relied on nonstandard units of measurement.

flashcards.definition: except if; used to introduce a condition that must be met for the main statement to be true
flashcards.translation: a menos que, exceto se — introduz uma condição que precisa ser cumprida
flashcards.example: A police office might say, “Step out of the car please, ma’am.” A customer service agent might say, “Can I help you, ma’am?” But I would caution you against using “ma’am” unless you’re sure it’s appropriate.
contexts: You can't enter the building unless you have a valid badge.
I won't go to the meeting unless they send the agenda in advance.

flashcards.definition: slightly too severe, unkind, or critical
flashcards.translation: um pouco severo, um tanto duro ou cruel demais
flashcards.example: In my opinion, this is a bit harsh.

flashcards.definition: done intentionally to create a particular impression or reaction in others
flashcards.translation: para causar efeito, intencionalmente para criar uma impressão em quem ouve
flashcards.example: You can use “ain’t” for effect.
contexts: He paused dramatically for effect before announcing the winner of the competition.
She lowered her voice for effect, making the story feel even more suspenseful.

flashcards.definition: to attract notice or interest, often suddenly or forcefully
flashcards.translation: chamar atenção, atrair o olhar ou o interesse das pessoas
flashcards.example: People sometimes purposely use “ain’t” just to grab attention.

flashcards.definition: to be spoken aloud; the phrase 'never cross our lips' means never to be said
flashcards.translation: sair da boca — 'nunca cruzar nossos lábios' = nunca ser dito
flashcards.example: It should never, ever, ever cross our lips.
contexts: That secret must never cross our lips outside of this room — not to anyone.
They made a pact that his name would never cross their lips again after what happened.

flashcards.definition: evidence or facts that confirm something is true
flashcards.translation: prova, evidência — fato ou dado que confirma que algo é verdade
flashcards.example: Here was proof that all the grown-ups in our lives had been lying to us the whole time.

flashcards.definition: adults; people who are fully mature (informal, often used from a child's perspective)
flashcards.translation: adultos — pessoas maduras (informal, geralmente do ponto de vista de uma criança)
flashcards.example: Here was proof that all the grown-ups in our lives had been lying to us the whole time.

flashcards.definition: more than two but not very many; a few
flashcards.translation: vários, alguns — mais de dois, mas não muitos
flashcards.example: Over a 60-year musical career, the singer won several Grammys, performed around the world, and was recognized as a cultural treasure of Brazil.
contexts: She visited several countries during her three-month trip through Southeast Asia.
There are several reasons why the launch was delayed, but the main one was a supply chain issue.

flashcards.definition: something very valuable or precious; to value something deeply and keep it safe (note: typo for 'treasure')
flashcards.translation: tesouro; algo muito valioso ou precioso; prezar, guardar com carinho
flashcards.example: Over a 60-year musical career, the singer won several Grammys, performed around the world, and was recognized as a cultural treasure of Brazil.

flashcards.definition: acknowledged officially; identified; given credit or appreciation
flashcards.translation: reconhecido — identificado; recebeu crédito ou homenagem por algo
flashcards.example: Over a 60-year musical career, the singer won several Grammys, performed around the world, and was recognized as a cultural treasure of Brazil.

flashcards.definition: quiet, relaxed, and not showy or intense; understated
flashcards.translation: discreto, tranquilo, sem muito alarde; suave e sem exageros
flashcards.example: But it was low-key : unlike jazz, which was high-energy , bossa nova was typically played at lower volumes and without a lot of drums and percussion instruments .

flashcards.definition: a flat piece of ceramic material used to cover bathroom walls or floors
flashcards.translation: azulejo de banheiro — peça cerâmica usada para revestir paredes e pisos
flashcards.example: The acoustics with the bathroom tile were just a bonus .

flashcards.definition: a large group of people gathered together; also used to describe a particular social group or scene
flashcards.translation: multidão, público; também: um grupo social específico (ex: a turma dos cafés)
flashcards.example: Bossa nova was the perfect style for the sophisticated crowd in the cafés and bars of Rio de Janeiro, which was undergoing a cultural renaissance at the time.

flashcards.definition: experiencing or going through a process of change
flashcards.translation: passando por, sofrendo — em processo de mudança ou transformação
flashcards.example: Bossa nova was the perfect style for the sophisticated crowd in the cafés and bars of Rio de Janeiro, which was undergoing a cultural renaissance at the time.

flashcards.definition: having a calming or relaxing effect; reducing stress or discomfort
flashcards.translation: calmante, relaxante — que acalma ou alivia o estresse

flashcards.definition: at any particular moment in time; at any specific instant
flashcards.translation: em qualquer momento específico, a qualquer instante
flashcards.example: There’s usually only one or at most two things going on in a song at any given point .
contexts: At any given point during rush hour, there are thousands of cars stuck on this highway.
The app can track up to one hundred active users at any given point without slowing down.

flashcards.definition: moving or being shared over a wider area or among more people
flashcards.translation: espalhando, se difundindo — alcançando mais pessoas ou lugares
flashcards.example: On one such tour, an American musician met Gilberto and started spreading the word once he got back home.

flashcards.definition: a sudden and dramatic success; a hit that brings someone or something to wider attention
flashcards.translation: um grande sucesso que lança alguém ao estrelato; momento de estouro
flashcards.example: It won the Grammy for record of the year in 1965 and included the breakout hit , “The Girl from Ipanema.” Like “Chega de Saudade,” “The Girl from Ipanema” had already been released.

flashcards.definition: stopped completely; came to an end
flashcards.translation: parou, foi interrompido — cessou completamente
flashcards.example: Bossa nova music’s development was halted in the 1960s by the Brazilian military dictatorship , which considered it an outside political or cultural influence .
contexts: Construction on the new bridge was halted due to the unexpected heavy rain.
The soldiers halted immediately when they heard the command echo across the field.

flashcards.definition: avoided social contact; preferred to be alone and private; did not share personal information
flashcards.translation: ficou para si mesmo, evitou contato social; era reservado e introspectivo
flashcards.example: A writer in a British newspaper said he was “a reclusive genius in a nation of extroverts .” On a personal level , Gilberto generally kept to himself .
contexts: He was a quiet, reserved man who kept to himself and rarely joined conversations at the office.
After the disagreement, she kept to herself for the rest of the week and avoided the group.

fill_blanks.sentence: All the major country music labels are ___ along Nashville’s Music Row.
Now, though, some artists are going the other way: they start as pop stars, and then ___ a country album.
First, speed—a café could serve ___ of customers an hour instead of making them wait.
Lana Del Rey has released two country songs ___ of an all-country album expected soon.
It’s not a ___; it’s not profanity.
They boiled water, poured it over ___, waited for the grounds to settle to the bottom, and sipped carefully.
They boiled water, ___ it over coffee grounds, waited for the grounds to settle to the bottom, and sipped carefully.
In the 1990s, for example, country singers Garth Brooks and Shania Twain ___ the top of the charts in both country and pop formats.
The Dust Bowl was a ten-year environmental disaster that ___ the Great Plains, in the middle of the country.

All the major country ___ are headquartered along Nashville’s Music Row.
But it was low-key : ___ jazz, which was high-energy , bossa nova was typically played at lower volumes and without a lot of drums and percussion instruments .
So when a ___ hit in the early 1930s, massive windstorms blew the loose topsoil all over the region, covering towns and farms in thick dust.
That became what we call “filter coffee” or “___.” Unlike espresso, which forces hot water under pressure, filter coffee just uses gravity.
But in the early 1920s, the federal government ___ to standardize national roadways.
Bossa nova was the perfect style for the sophisticated ___ in the cafés and bars of Rio de Janeiro, which was undergoing a cultural renaissance at the time.
She put the coffee grounds on top of the paper, poured hot water over the grounds, and watched as a ___ beverage dripped out the bottom and into her cup.
Here was proof that all the ___ in our lives had been lying to us the whole time.
It introduced country themes to millions of music fans who might never have given country a ___.
The machine forced hot water under pressure through ___ coffee.

multiple_choice.question: Now, they would have a ___, easy-to-use system of national highways.

The American jazz singer Stan Getz collaborated with Gilberto on an album called, imaginatively, “Getz/Gilberto,” which was a huge international success and included several songs that would become ___ jazz songs of the era.

Dolly Parton said goodbye to someone she loved but had to ___.
The genre has its own ___ and dedicated radio stations.
So when a ___ hit in the early 1930s, massive windstorms blew the loose topsoil all over the region, covering towns and farms in thick dust.
So she took a page out of her son’s school notebook and ___ it into a cone.
It’s the female equivalent of the word “sir.” This can often be considered polite and respectful, but it always ___ an emotional distance between two people.
Lana Del Rey has released two country songs ___ of an all-country album expected soon.
People didn’t like the ___ of grounds in the bottom of their cups, and it took a long time to make.
The style ___ across Europe and eventually around the world.
Luke Combs ___ when his interpretation of “Fast Car” became an international hit.
Country is a genre rooted in acoustic instruments and stories about everyday life—love, loss, and small- town ___.
You can certainly find country music stars ___ arenas.
The machine forced hot water under pressure through ___ coffee.
